# Sof Zman Achilas Chametz

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Sof Zman Achilas Chametz Baal Hatanya

The latest time one is allowed to eat chametz on Erev Pesach according to the Baal Hatanya. This time is identical to Sof zman tfilah Baal Hatanya.

It is 4 Baal Hatanya shaos zmaniyos into the day. The day is calculated from netz amiti, sunrise, to sunset, so this time is 4 Baal Hatanya shaos zmaniyos after netz amiti.

This zman is only for Erev Pesach; on other days it may not be available.

It may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzBaalHatanya`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzBaalHatanya`

## Sof Zman Achilas Chametz GRA

The latest time one is allowed to eat chametz on Erev Pesach according to the opinion of the [GRA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilna_Gaon). This time is identical to Sof zman tfilah GRA.

It is 4 GRA shaos zmaniyos into the day. The day is calculated from sunrise to sunset, so this time is 4 GRA shaos zmaniyos after sea-level sunrise.

This zman is only for Erev Pesach; on other days it may not be available.

It may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzGRA`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzGRA`

## Sof Zman Achilas Chametz MGA 16 Point 1 Degrees

The latest time one is allowed to eat chametz on Erev Pesach according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), based on alos being when the sun is 16.1° below the horizon before sunrise.

It is 4 shaos zmaniyos after dawn. The day is calculated from dawn to nightfall, with both set at 16.1° below sunrise or sunset.

This zman is only for Erev Pesach; on other days it may not be available.

It may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as northern and southern locations even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzMGA16Point1Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzMGA16Point1Degrees`

## Sof Zman Achilas Chametz MGA 72 Minutes

The latest time one is allowed to eat chametz on Erev Pesach according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), based on alos being 72 minutes before sunrise. This time is identical to Sof zman tfilah MGA 72 minutes.

It is 4 shaos zmaniyos after dawn. The day is calculated from dawn 72 minutes before sunrise to nightfall 72 minutes after sunset.

This zman is only for Erev Pesach; on other days it may not be available.

It may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzMGA72Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzMGA72Minutes`

## Sof Zman Achilas Chametz MGA 72 Minutes Zmanis

The latest time one is allowed to eat chametz on Erev Pesach according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), based on alos being 72 zmaniyos minutes before sunrise. This time is identical to Sof zman tfilah MGA 72 minutes zmanis.

It is 4 shaos zmaniyos after dawn. The day is calculated from dawn 72 minutes zmanis before sunrise to nightfall 72 minutes zmanis after sunset.

This zman is only for Erev Pesach; on other days it may not be available.

It may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzMGA72MinutesZmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanAchilasChametzMGA72MinutesZmanis`
